title: CASEOVERLAP warning for a priority case
body:
With the following code,
```systemverilog
module cases(input logic [1:0]  foo_i,
             output logic [2:0] bar_o);

  always_comb begin
    priority casez (foo_i)
      2'b ?1: bar_o = 3'd0;
      2'b 1?: bar_o = 3'd1;
      default: bar_o = 3'd2;
    endcase
  end

endmodule
```
verilator generates a CASEOVERLAP warning:
```
$ /opt/verilator-test/bin/verilator --lint-only cases.sv
%Warning-CASEOVERLAP: cases.sv:7:7: Case values overlap (example pattern 0x3)
    7 |       2'b 1?: bar_o = 3'd1;
      |       ^~~~~~
                      ... Use "/* verilator lint_off CASEOVERLAP */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 warning(s)
```

The cases do indeed overlap, but that's kind of the whole point of the `priority` keyword in the spec. Maybe this warning needs disabling for `priority-case` expressions?

Tested with current head of master:
```
$ /opt/verilator-test/bin/verilator --version
Verilator 4.201 devel rev v4.200-38-g2e158d88
```
